subject: feat(a11y): wire focus hooks into HeaderFilters + search modal
body:
HeaderFilters: one useDisclosureFocus per trigger (fuel + radius),
shared useFocusTrap on the dropdown panel. Each close returns focus
to the correct button. Rapid fuel↔radius switching produces a harmless
one-commit bounce through the outgoing trigger before landing on the
new panel.

Search modal (app/(tabs)/index.tsx): useDisclosureFocus + useFocusTrap
on the modal. firstItemRef attaches to LocationSearch's TextInput via
a new inputRef prop, so the user can type immediately on open.
triggerRef is left unattached — the search button lives in
HeaderFilters, so on close focus returns to document.body rather than
the button. Threading the trigger ref through SearchModalProvider
could close that gap if it becomes a noticeable miss.

Remaining disclosures (TutorialModal, InstallInstructionsModal) are
captured in docs/TODO.md — they have trickier focus-return semantics
(opened from inside the burger menu, TutorialModal also auto-opens on
first launch).

Tags: a11y, keyboard, hooks
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: a11y-polish
Semver: patch
